✦ project // case study

MIA

iOS app helping aphasia patients communicate

category mobile app
status in development
role Solo Developer — Design & Development
timeline Ongoing personal project
tech stack:
Swift Xcode iOS Storyboard
Links coming soon
📱 TODO: Add hero screenshot

📋 project_overview.md

MIA (Mobile Interface for Aphasia) is an iOS application I developed to help patients with aphasia communicate more effectively. Aphasia is a language disorder that affects a person’s ability to communicate, often resulting from stroke or brain injury. This app provides visual and audio aids to help patients express their needs and communicate with caregivers and family.

❓ the_problem.txt

Patients with aphasia often struggle to communicate basic needs and emotions, leading to frustration for both them and their caregivers. Existing solutions are often expensive, complicated, or not designed with accessibility in mind.

💡 the_solution.txt

I designed MIA to be intuitive and accessible, featuring large visual icons, simple navigation, and audio feedback. The app includes categories for common needs like food, comfort, emotions, and activities, making it easy for users to express themselves with just a few taps.

✨ features.list

Visual Communication

Large, clear icons representing common needs and phrases

Audio Feedback

Text-to-speech functionality to help convey messages

Customizable Categories

Ability to add custom phrases and images

Simple Navigation

Designed for users with motor or cognitive difficulties

🧩 challenges.log

challenge:

Learning Swift from Scratch

Taught myself Swift and Xcode with no prior iOS experience

solution:

Used online resources, documentation, and iterative practice

challenge:

Accessibility Design

Creating an interface usable by people with various impairments

solution:

Research on accessible design patterns and user testing

🎯 outcomes.results

  • Successfully built a functional iOS prototype
  • Gained proficiency in Swift and Xcode
  • Developed understanding of accessible design principles

🚀 next_steps.md

Continue development with planned features including cloud sync for settings, caregiver mode for easy customization, and expanded language support.
